duration: Seen for only a second; heard singing periodically throughout 20 minute period.
description: Heard singing regularly between 6:40am and 7:00am from open habitat southwest of the Audubon center parking lot very soon after getting out of car. Series of rapid “zeets”, increasing in speed and rising upwards toward the end (sound recorded). Only got one distant look at the bird, so couldn’t make out much detail, but I did see a small, entirely yellow warbler with dark streaking on flanks, ruling out Yellow Warbler.  Went silent around 7:00am and as unable to relocate through 7:30, when I had to leave.In the sound recording uploaded to this report, the bird's song is a little faint, so volume should be turned up high. One bout of song is heard between seconds 3 and 4.5. The recording has also been uploaded to eBird, which shows a spectogram of the song, here: https://macaulaylibrary.org/asset/449800471.
photographer: Photographer: Michael Lester, Mark Peterson, Michael Lester, Michael Lester, Michael Lester
